sentences.en: It isn't easy being a professional athlete.
sentences.zh: 当一名职业运动员并不容易。

grammar.type: 形式主语 it + 动名词
grammar.note: It isn't easy 中 it 为形式主语，真正的主语是后面的动名词短语 being a professional athlete；这种“It is + 形容词 + doing”结构常用来对某种经历下判断。

sentences.en: Not only are the physical demands greater than most people could handle, athletes also face intense psychological pressure during competition.
sentences.zh: 不仅其身体上的要求之高超出了大多数人所能承受的范围，运动员在比赛中还要面对巨大的心理压力。

grammar.type: Not only 引导的部分倒装 + 比较级
grammar.note: Not only 置于句首，前半句发生部分倒装 are the physical demands greater...；greater than most people could handle 为比较结构；后半句 athletes also face... 与之呼应，构成“不仅……而且……”。

sentences.en: This is something that British tennis player Emma Raducanu wrote about on social media following her withdrawal from the 2021 Wimbledon tournament.
sentences.zh: 这正是英国网球运动员艾玛·拉杜卡努在退出2021年温布尔登网球赛后在社交媒体上所谈及的事情。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 现在分词短语作状语
grammar.note: 主干是 This is something；that... wrote about 为定语从句修饰 something，关系代词 that 作 wrote about 的宾语；following her withdrawal... 为介词短语作时间状语，following 意为“在……之后”。

sentences.en: Though the young player had been doing well in the tournament, she began having difficulty regulating her breathing and heart rate during a match, which she later attributed to 'the accumulation of the excitement and the buzz'.
sentences.zh: 尽管这位年轻选手在赛事中一直表现不错，但她在一场比赛中开始难以调节自己的呼吸和心率，她后来把这归因于“兴奋与激动的累积”。

grammar.type: Though 让步状语从句 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: Though 引导让步状语从句 the young player had been doing well...（过去完成进行时）；主句是 she began having difficulty regulating...，have difficulty (in) doing 表“做某事有困难”；which 引导非限制性定语从句指代前面整件事，attribute A to B 表“把A归因于B”。

sentences.en: For athletes, some level of performance stress is almost unavoidable.
sentences.zh: 对运动员而言，某种程度的表现压力几乎是不可避免的。

grammar.type: 主系表
grammar.note: For athletes 为介词短语作状语，限定范围；主干是 some level of performance stress is almost unavoidable；some level of 表“某种程度的”。

sentences.en: But there are many different factors that dictate just how people's minds and bodies respond to stressful events.
sentences.zh: 但有许多不同的因素决定着人们的身心究竟如何应对充满压力的事件。

grammar.type: There be + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 there are many different factors；that dictate... 为定语从句修饰 factors；从句宾语为 how 引导的宾语从句 how people's minds and bodies respond to...，just 起加强语气作用。

sentences.en: Typically, stress is the result of an exchange between two factors: demands and resources.
sentences.zh: 通常，压力是两个因素之间相互作用的结果：需求与资源。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 冒号说明
grammar.note: 主干是 stress is the result of an exchange between two factors；Typically 为副词作状语；冒号后 demands and resources 具体点明是哪两个因素。

sentences.en: An athlete may feel stressed about an event if they feel the demands on them are greater than they can handle.
sentences.zh: 如果运动员觉得加在自己身上的要求超出了自己所能应付的范围，就可能对某项赛事感到有压力。

grammar.type: if 条件状语从句 + 省略引导词的宾语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 An athlete may feel stressed about an event；if 引导条件状语从句 if they feel...；feel 后接省略了 that 的宾语从句 the demands on them are greater than they can handle，其中含 than 比较结构。

sentences.en: These demands include the high level of physical and mental effort required to succeed, and also the athlete's concerns about the difficulty of the event, their chance of succeeding, and any potential dangers such as injury.
sentences.zh: 这些要求包括取得成功所需的高强度体力与脑力付出，也包括运动员对赛事难度、自己获胜机会以及诸如受伤等任何潜在危险的担忧。

grammar.type: 并列宾语 + 过去分词后置定语
grammar.note: 主干是 These demands include A, and also B。A 是 the high level of physical and mental effort，required to succeed 为过去分词短语作后置定语；B 是 the athlete's concerns about...，其后 the difficulty / their chance / any potential dangers 为 about 的三个并列宾语。

sentences.en: Resources, on the other hand, are a person's ability to cope with these demands.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，资源指的是一个人应对这些要求的能力。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 插入语
grammar.note: 主干是 Resources are a person's ability；on the other hand 为插入语，与上文 demands 形成对照；to cope with these demands 为不定式作后置定语修饰 ability。

sentences.en: These include factors such as the competitor's degree of confidence, how much they believe they can control the situation's outcome, and whether they're looking forward to the event or not.
sentences.zh: 这些资源包括诸如选手的自信程度、他们在多大程度上相信自己能掌控局面的结果，以及他们是否期待这项赛事等因素。

grammar.type: 并列宾语（名词短语 + 两个从句）
grammar.note: 主干是 These include factors；such as 后接三个并列成分：名词短语 the competitor's degree of confidence、how much 引导的从句、以及 whether...or not 引导的从句，三者共同举例说明 factors。

sentences.en: Each new demand or change in circumstances affects whether a person responds positively or negatively to stress.
sentences.zh: 每一项新的要求或情况的变化，都会影响一个人对压力做出积极还是消极的反应。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + whether 宾语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 Each new demand or change...affects...；affects 的宾语是 whether 引导的宾语从句 whether a person responds positively or negatively to stress；in circumstances 修饰 change。

sentences.en: Typically, the more resources a person feels they have in handling the situation, the more positive their stress response.
sentences.zh: 通常，一个人觉得自己在应对局面时拥有的资源越多，其压力反应就越积极。

grammar.type: the more...the more 比例状语结构
grammar.note: 全句为“the + 比较级……, the + 比较级……”结构，表“越……就越……”；前半 the more resources a person feels they have...（a person feels 为插入的主谓）；后半 the more positive their stress response 省略了系动词 is。

sentences.en: This positive stress response is called a challenge state.
sentences.zh: 这种积极的压力反应被称为“挑战状态”。

grammar.type: 被动语态
grammar.note: 主干是 This positive stress response is called a challenge state，为“be called + 名词”被动结构，a challenge state 为主语补足语，给这种反应命名。

words.def: 挑战状态（积极的压力反应）

sentences.en: But should the person feel there are too many demands placed on them, the more likely they are to experience a negative stress response – known as a threat state.
sentences.zh: 但如果这个人觉得加在自己身上的要求太多，他们就越有可能经历一种消极的压力反应——即所谓的“威胁状态”。

grammar.type: should 引导的倒装条件句 + 过去分词短语
grammar.note: should the person feel... 是省略 if 的倒装条件句，等于 if the person should feel...；主句 the more likely they are to experience... 表结果；placed on them 为过去分词短语修饰 demands；破折号后 known as a threat state 为过去分词短语补充命名。

words.def: 威胁状态（消极的压力反应）

sentences.en: Research shows that the challenge states lead to good performance, while threat states lead to poorer performance.
sentences.zh: 研究表明，挑战状态会带来良好的表现，而威胁状态则会导致较差的表现。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + while 对比状语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 Research shows that...，that 引导宾语从句；从句内 the challenge states lead to good performance 为主句，while 引导对比状语从句 threat states lead to poorer performance，两者形成对照。

sentences.en: So, in Emma Raducanu's case, a much larger audience, higher expectations and facing a more skilful opponent, may all have led her to feel there were greater demands being placed on her at Wimbledon – but she didn't have the resources to tackle them.
sentences.zh: 因此，就艾玛·拉杜卡努而言，多得多的观众、更高的期望，以及要面对一个更有技巧的对手，可能都使她觉得温布尔登赛场上加在她身上的要求更大了——但她并没有应对这些要求的资源。

grammar.type: 并列主语 + 情态完成式 + 破折号转折
grammar.note: 主语为三个并列成分 a much larger audience, higher expectations and facing a more skilful opponent；谓语 may all have led her to feel... 为“情态动词 + have done”表对过去的推测；feel 后接省略 that 的宾语从句，being placed 为现在分词被动修饰 demands；破折号后 but 引出转折。

sentences.en: This led to her experiencing a threat response.
sentences.zh: 这导致她经历了一种威胁反应。

grammar.type: 动名词复合结构
grammar.note: 主干是 This led to...；led to 后接动名词复合结构 her experiencing a threat response，其中 her 为动名词 experiencing 的逻辑主语。

sentences.en: Our challenge and threat responses essentially influence how our body responds to stressful situations, as both affect the production of adrenaline and cortisol – also known as 'stress hormones'.
sentences.zh: 我们的挑战反应与威胁反应从根本上影响着身体如何应对压力情境，因为二者都会影响肾上腺素和皮质醇的分泌——它们也被称为“压力激素”。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 宾语从句 + as 原因状语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 Our challenge and threat responses...influence how our body responds...，how 引导宾语从句；as 引导原因状语从句 both affect the production of adrenaline and cortisol；破折号后 also known as 'stress hormones' 为过去分词短语补充说明。

sentences.en: During a challenge state, adrenaline increases the amount of blood pumped from the heart and expands the blood vessels, which allows more energy to be delivered to the muscles and brain.
sentences.zh: 在挑战状态下，肾上腺素会增加从心脏泵出的血量并扩张血管，这使得更多能量得以输送到肌肉和大脑。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干为并列谓语 adrenaline increases the amount of blood... and expands the blood vessels；pumped from the heart 为过去分词短语修饰 blood；which 引导非限制性定语从句指代前面整件事，allow sth to be done 表“使某物被……”。

sentences.en: This increase of blood and decrease of pressure in the blood vessels has been consistently related to superior sport performance in everything from cricket batting, to golf putting and football penalty taking.
sentences.zh: 血管中血量的增加与压力的降低，一直被认为与更出色的运动表现相关，涉及从板球击球、高尔夫推杆到足球罚点球的方方面面。

grammar.type: 现在完成时被动 + from...to... 列举
grammar.note: 主干是 This increase...and decrease...has been consistently related to superior sport performance，为现在完成时被动，主语由 increase 和 decrease 两个并列名词构成故谓语视其为整体；in everything from A, to B and C 用 from...to... 列举多个运动项目。

sentences.en: But during a threat state, cortisol inhibits the positive effect of adrenaline, resulting in tighter blood vessels, higher blood pressure, slower psychological responses, and a faster heart rate.
sentences.zh: 但在威胁状态下，皮质醇会抑制肾上腺素的积极作用，从而导致血管收缩、血压升高、心理反应变慢以及心率加快。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 现在分词短语作结果状语
grammar.note: 主干是 cortisol inhibits the positive effect of adrenaline；resulting in... 为现在分词短语作结果状语，其后 tighter blood vessels / higher blood pressure / slower psychological responses / a faster heart rate 为 in 的四个并列宾语。

sentences.en: In short, a threat state makes people more anxious – they make worse decisions and perform more poorly.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，威胁状态会让人更加焦虑——他们会做出更糟的决定，表现也更差。

grammar.type: 使役结构 + 破折号补充说明
grammar.note: In short 为插入语表总结；主干是 a threat state makes people more anxious，为“make + 宾语 + 形容词”使役结构；破折号后 they make worse decisions and perform more poorly 进一步说明后果。

sentences.en: In tennis players, cortisol has been associated with more unsuccessful serves and greater anxiety.
sentences.zh: 在网球运动员身上，皮质醇一直被认为与更多失败的发球以及更强的焦虑相关联。

grammar.type: 现在完成时被动
grammar.note: 主干是 cortisol has been associated with...，为现在完成时被动，be associated with 表“与……相关联”；more unsuccessful serves and greater anxiety 为 with 的两个并列宾语。

sentences.en: That said, anxiety is also a common experience for athletes when they're under pressure.
sentences.zh: 话虽如此，焦虑也是运动员在承受压力时的一种常见体验。

grammar.type: 独立结构 That said + 时间状语从句
grammar.note: That said 为独立结构，意为“话虽如此、尽管这么说”，起让步过渡作用；主干是 anxiety is also a common experience for athletes；when 引导时间状语从句 when they're under pressure。

sentences.en: Anxiety can increase heart rate and perspiration, cause heart palpitations, muscle tremors and shortness of breath, as well as headaches, nausea, stomach pain, weakness and a desire to escape in more extreme cases.
sentences.zh: 焦虑会加快心率、增加出汗，引发心悸、肌肉颤抖和呼吸急促，在更极端的情况下还会引起头痛、恶心、胃痛、乏力以及想要逃离的冲动。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 大量并列宾语
grammar.note: 主干为并列谓语 Anxiety can increase... and cause...；increase 后接 heart rate and perspiration；cause 后接 heart palpitations, muscle tremors and shortness of breath, as well as headaches...等一长串并列宾语；in more extreme cases 为条件状语。

sentences.en: Anxiety can also reduce concentration and self-control and cause overthinking.
sentences.zh: 焦虑还会削弱注意力和自控力，并导致过度思虑。

grammar.type: 并列谓语
grammar.note: 主干为并列谓语 Anxiety can also reduce... and cause...；reduce 的宾语是 concentration and self-control 两个并列名词，cause 的宾语是 overthinking。

sentences.en: The intensity with which a person experiences anxiety depends on the demands and resources they have.
sentences.zh: 一个人体验焦虑的强烈程度，取决于他们所拥有的需求与资源。

grammar.type: 介词 + which 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 The intensity...depends on the demands and resources；with which a person experiences anxiety 为“介词 + which”引导的定语从句修饰 intensity；they have 为省略关系词的定语从句修饰 the demands and resources。

sentences.en: Anxiety may also manifest itself in the form of excitement or nervousness depending on the stress response.
sentences.zh: 焦虑也可能以兴奋或紧张的形式表现出来，具体取决于压力反应。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 现在分词短语作状语
grammar.note: 主干是 Anxiety may also manifest itself，manifest itself 表“显现、表现出来”；in the form of excitement or nervousness 说明表现形式；depending on the stress response 为现在分词短语作状语，表“视……而定”。

sentences.en: Negative stress responses can be damaging to both physical and mental health – and repeated episodes of anxiety coupled with negative responses can increase risk of heart disease and depression.
sentences.zh: 消极的压力反应会损害身心健康——而反复发作的焦虑加上消极反应，会增加患心脏病和抑郁症的风险。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 过去分词短语
grammar.note: 破折号连接两个分句。前句主干 Negative stress responses can be damaging to...；后句主干 repeated episodes of anxiety...can increase risk...，coupled with negative responses 为过去分词短语修饰 episodes of anxiety，表“加上、伴随”。

sentences.en: But there are many ways athletes can ensure they respond positively under pressure.
sentences.zh: 但运动员有许多办法可以确保自己在压力下做出积极反应。

grammar.type: There be + 省略关系词的定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 there are many ways；athletes can ensure... 为省略关系副词的定语从句修饰 ways；ensure 后又接省略 that 的宾语从句 they respond positively under pressure。

sentences.en: Positive stress responses can be promoted through the language that they and others – such as coaches or parents – use.
sentences.zh: 积极的压力反应可以通过他们自己以及他人——比如教练或父母——所使用的语言来加以促进。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 定语从句 + 破折号插入语
grammar.note: 主干是 Positive stress responses can be promoted through the language，为情态被动；that they and others...use 为定语从句修饰 language；破折号之间 such as coaches or parents 为插入语，举例说明 others。

sentences.en: Psychologists can also help athletes change how they see their physiological responses – such as helping them see a higher heart rate as excitement, rather than nerves.
sentences.zh: 心理学家还能帮助运动员改变他们看待自身生理反应的方式——比如帮助他们把更快的心率看作兴奋，而不是紧张。

grammar.type: help sb do + 宾语从句 + 破折号举例
grammar.note: 主干是 Psychologists can also help athletes change...，help sb do 结构；change 的宾语是 how 引导的宾语从句 how they see their physiological responses；破折号后 such as helping them see...as..., rather than... 举例，see A as B 表“把A视作B”。

sentences.en: Developing psychological skills, such as visualisation, can also help decrease physiological responses to threat.
sentences.zh: 培养诸如想象（观想）之类的心理技能，也有助于减弱对威胁的生理反应。

grammar.type: 动名词短语作主语
grammar.note: 主语是动名词短语 Developing psychological skills，such as visualisation 为插入的举例；谓语 can also help decrease...，help (to) do 结构；to threat 修饰 physiological responses。

words.def: 想象；观想（在头脑中构想画面）

sentences.en: Visualisation may involve the athlete recreating a mental picture of a time when they performed well, or picturing themselves doing well in the future.
sentences.zh: 想象可能包括运动员在头脑中重现自己曾经表现出色时的画面，或者设想自己将来表现良好。

grammar.type: 动名词复合结构 + 并列
grammar.note: 主干是 Visualisation may involve...；involve 后接动名词复合结构 the athlete recreating...，其中 the athlete 为逻辑主语；when they performed well 为定语从句修饰 a time；or 连接并列的 picturing themselves doing well...。

sentences.en: This can help create a feeling of control over the stressful event.
sentences.zh: 这有助于营造一种对压力事件的掌控感。

grammar.type: help do 结构
grammar.note: 主干是 This can help create a feeling，help (to) create 结构；of control over the stressful event 为介词短语作后置定语修饰 a feeling，说明是何种感觉。

sentences.en: Recreating competitive pressure during training can also help athletes learn how to deal with stress.
sentences.zh: 在训练中重现比赛的压力，也能帮助运动员学会如何应对压力。

grammar.type: 动名词短语作主语 + help sb do
grammar.note: 主语是动名词短语 Recreating competitive pressure during training；谓语 can also help athletes learn...，help sb do 结构；learn 后接 how to deal with stress 这一“疑问词 + 不定式”结构作宾语。

sentences.en: An example of this might be scoring athletes against their peers to create a sense of competition.
sentences.zh: 这方面的一个例子，可能是让运动员与同伴相互打分比拼，以营造一种竞争氛围。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 动名词作表语 + 不定式目的
grammar.note: 主干是 An example of this might be scoring athletes...，动名词短语 scoring athletes against their peers 作表语；against their peers 表“与同伴相较”；to create a sense of competition 为不定式表目的。

sentences.en: This would increase the demands which players experience compared to a normal training session, while still allowing them to practise coping with stress.
sentences.zh: 与普通的训练课相比，这会增加选手所感受到的要求，同时仍让他们能够练习应对压力。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 过去分词短语 + while 伴随状语
grammar.note: 主干是 This would increase the demands；which players experience 为定语从句修饰 demands；compared to a normal training session 为过去分词短语作状语表比较；while still allowing them to practise... 为现在分词短语作伴随状语，practise doing 表“练习做某事”。

questions.title: Questions 14-18 · Paragraph matching
instructions: Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs, A-F.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 14-18 on your answer sheet.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

items.prompt: reference to two chemical compounds which impact on performance
examples of strategies for minimising the effects of stress
how a sportsperson accounted for their own experience of stress
study results indicating links between stress responses and performance
mention of people who can influence how athletes perceive their stress responses

questions.title: Questions 19-22 · Sentence completion
instructions: Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 19-22 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: Performance stress involves many demands on the athlete, for example, coping with the possible risk of 19 ____ .
Cortisol can cause tennis players to produce fewer good 20 ____ .
Psychologists can help athletes to view their physiological responses as the effect of a positive feeling such as 21 ____ .
22 ____ is an example of a psychological technique which can reduce an athlete's stress responses.

questions.title: Questions 23 and 24 · Choose TWO letters
